summary: The proposal emphasizes the organization of known data, but neglects the empirical rigor of physical measurement.
body: The proposal for SciAtlas describes a method for organizing scientific knowledge through a graph. However, it does not specify how the system accounts for the physical measurement of new substances. In our work with pitchblende, the evidence was not found in existing records, but in the painstaking isolation of the new substance through fractionation. I wish to know how this automated system distinguishes a theoretical correlation from the kind of evidence which chemical science demands: the precise determination of an atomic weight.
